name: compliance sync mechanism: distinct jurisdictions land in distinct namespace folders (us-government vs european-union)
command:
mkdir -p .claude-tdd-pro generated-code-quality-standards && printf -- '- id: nist-csf-2
  name: NIST CSF 2
  url: https://www.nist.gov/cyberframework
  authoritative_publisher: NIST
  jurisdiction: us-government
  applicable_to: [cybersecurity]
  identifier_scheme: nist-framework
  why_authoritative: |
    Federal mandate.
    Authoritative.
    Widely adopted.
  fetch_frequency: weekly
  legal_review_required: false
  paywalled: false
- id: eu-ai-act
  name: EU AI Act
  url: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/1689
  authoritative_publisher: European Parliament
  jurisdiction: european-union
  applicable_to: [ai-systems]
  identifier_scheme: eu-regulation
  why_authoritative: |
    Adopted by EU legislators.
    Binding on EU operators.
    Authoritative EU AI source.
  fetch_frequency: weekly
  legal_review_required: true
  paywalled: false
' > .claude-tdd-pro/COMPLIANCE-URLS.yaml && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/compliance/sync-from-sources.sh" --catalog "$PWD/.claude-tdd-pro/COMPLIANCE-URLS.yaml" --target "$PWD/generated-code-quality-standards" && test -d generated-code-quality-standards/us-government/nist-csf-2 && test -d generated-code-quality-standards/european-union/eu-ai-act && echo ok 1>&2
